condition: DiagnosticReport.conclusion is non-empty or DiagnosticReport.presentedForm contains text-extractable attachments
narrative_md: When a DiagnosticReport carries free-text content -- either a conclusion string or one or more presentedForm attachments -- a note row is created to preserve the unstructured clinical narrative. This is independent of the domain-routed rows (measurement, observation, procedure_occurrence) that are produced from the report's LOINC code and conclusionCode. A single DiagnosticReport may produce both a domain-routed row and a note row. No reference implementation currently maps DiagnosticReport.conclusion/presentedForm to the OMOP note table; omoponfhir's DocumentReference mapper is the closest reference.

fields.notes: Surrogate key. Hash/sequence of DiagnosticReport.id + content source indicator (conclusion vs presentedForm index). Must be unique across all note sources.
Resolve Patient/{id} reference to integer person_id. Skip row if unresolvable.
Date component of effectiveDateTime, or effectivePeriod.start. If absent, fall back to DiagnosticReport.issued (instant). Skip resource if no date available.
Full timestamp from effectiveDateTime, effectivePeriod.start, or issued. Set time to midnight if not given.
Maps the report's provenance/origin. Use 32817 (EHR) as the default. This field captures where the note came from (the source system), not what kind of note it is (that is note_class_concept_id).

sources.comment: Set from DocumentReference.type via LOINC→NoteType enum mapping (OmopNoteTypeMapping); defaults to concept from type lookup
Field listed as required but set to null in skeleton mapping

fields.notes: Maps the report category to a LOINC Document Type concept per OMOP CDM specification. Map LAB -> 44814645 (Note), RAD -> 44814641 (Radiology report), PAT -> 44814642 (Pathology report). 0 if unmappable.

sources.comment: Defaults to Concept(0); note_class_concept_id is technically non-compliant when 0 (required field)
Field listed as required but set to null in skeleton mapping

fields.notes: Display text of the report's primary code (typically LOINC). E.g. 'History and physical note'. Truncate to 250 characters. Fall back to DiagnosticReport.code.text or the LOINC code itself.

For conclusion: use the string directly. For presentedForm: base64-decode the data field. Only text/plain content types stored directly; other types require extraction or conversion. If both exist, create separate note rows or concatenate.

sources.comment: Maps DocumentReference.content.attachment.data (text/plain only); rejects non-text/plain; concatenates multiple attachments
Field listed but set to null; example shows Synthea presentedForm text/plain base64-encoded content
BuildNote method exists but note creation is commented out (//TMP: NOTE)

fields.notes: 32678 = UTF-8. FHIR strings are always UTF-8. For presentedForm, check contentType and encoding; default to 32678 for text/plain.

Map BCP-47/ISO 639 language codes to OMOP concepts descended from 4182347 (World Languages). en/en-US -> 4180186, de -> 4182948, fr -> 4181536, es -> 4182511. Fall back to DiagnosticReport.language. 0 if absent.
First performer reference resolved to provider_id. Filter to Practitioner-typed references. resultsInterpreter[0] is an alternative source.

sources.comment: Field listed but set to null; no performer resolution

fields.notes: Resolve Encounter/{id} to visit_occurrence_id. If unresolvable, log warning but still create the note row with null.

sources.comment: Field listed but set to null; no encounter resolution

fields.notes: Leave null unless visit details are modeled.
The source value mapped to note_class_concept_id. Store the raw category code (e.g. 'LAB', 'RAD', 'PAT').
If the note should be linked to a corresponding domain-routed row (e.g. the measurement_id or procedure_occurrence_id generated from the same DiagnosticReport), store that row's primary key here.

The CONCEPT_ID identifying which table note_event_id references. Use: 1147138 (measurement.measurement_id), 1147127 (observation.observation_id), 1147082 (procedure_occurrence.procedure_occurrence_id). Leave null if note_event_id is null.

entries.notes: Default for DiagnosticReport. Reports typically originate from electronic health record systems.
Only standard encoding concept defined in OMOP vocabulary. FHIR strings are always UTF-8.

references.notes: Primary reference for DiagnosticReport mapping, but does not produce note rows. Only handles conclusionCode for domain routing.
Skeleton with all 16 note fields listed but all set to null. Contains example DiagnosticReport with presentedForm base64-encoded text/plain content.
Closest reference for FHIR narrative to OMOP note mapping. Maps DocumentReference (not DiagnosticReport) with subject, author, encounter, attachment data (text/plain only).
10 Note Type to LOINC concept mappings.
Note entity exists, BuildNote method at line 700, but note creation is commented out (//TMP: NOTE at line 358).
HL7 official Note logical model with all 16 fields. No FML transformation rules for DiagnosticReport->note.

edge_cases.case: Empty conclusion (empty string or whitespace only)
edge_cases.handling: Do not create a note row. note_text is required and must be non-empty. Check conclusion.trim().length > 0 before creating the row.
Skip the attachment for note creation. Log a warning. OMOP note_text is designed for free text, not binary content. Consider extracting text from PDFs with a dedicated parser.
Create one note row per text-extractable attachment, each with a unique note_id. Differentiate via note_id generation strategy (hash of DiagnosticReport.id + attachment index).
OMOP note_text is varchar(MAX). However, downstream NLP tools may have size limits. Consider truncating or splitting at a configurable maximum with a log warning.
Assume text/plain if the data field is present and decodable as valid UTF-8 text. Otherwise skip the attachment.
The attachment references an external document. Either fetch the content at ETL time and inline it, or skip the attachment with a log warning.
Skip the attachment and log an error. The data field may be corrupted or not valid base64.
Attempt to detect encoding and transcode to UTF-8 before storing. Set encoding_concept_id = 32678 after successful transcoding. If transcoding fails, use 0.
Each note row should reflect the language of its specific attachment. Do not assume all attachments share the same language.

edge_cases.case: Status is preliminary, registered, or entered-in-error
edge_cases.handling: ETL-German rejects non-final statuses. For notes, preliminary reports may still contain valuable narrative. Consider accepting preliminary notes but tagging them. Reject entered-in-error.
Not supported. OMOP requires a single person_id. Skip the resource.
No date available. OMOP note_date is required. Skip the resource and log a warning.

edge_cases.case: DiagnosticReport has conclusion but no conclusionCode
edge_cases.handling: Still create a note row. The conclusion text is independent of the structured conclusionCode. A report can have a textual interpretation without coded findings.
Strip HTML tags to produce plain text for note_text, or store HTML as-is for NLP pipelines that can process it. Document the approach in ETL conventions.

edge_cases.case: Both conclusion and presentedForm text exist
edge_cases.handling: Option A (recommended): Create two separate note rows. Option B: Concatenate with delimiter. Option C: Prefer presentedForm as primary note, store conclusion in note_title.
